votes.reason: The Python code correctly identifies all valid elements by counting occurrences and checking the conditions, then verifies if the given answer is the maximum valid element or -1 if none exist, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly computes the counts of elements, identifies valid elements according to the Coq specification, and checks if the given answer is the maximum valid element or -1 if none exist.

The Python code correctly computes the counts of elements, identifies valid elements according to the Coq specification, and checks if the given answer is the maximum valid element or -1 if none exist.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it counts occurrences, identifies valid x values (x > 0 and count >= x), and returns the maximum valid x or -1 if none exist. This matches the Coq spec which requires ans to be the greatest valid element or -1 if no valid elements exist.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it counts occurrences, identifies valid x (where x > 0 and count(x) >= x), and returns the maximum valid x or -1 if none exist. This matches the Coq spec which requires ans to be the greatest valid element or -1 if no valid elements exist.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it counts occurrences, identifies valid x values (x > 0 and count(x) >= x), and returns true iff ans equals the maximum valid x (or -1 if none exist). This matches the Coq spec which requires ans to be a valid element that is >= all other valid elements, or -1 if no valid elements exist.

Both compute whether ans equals the maximum positive x such that x occurs at least x times in the list, or -1 if no such x exists. Python counts occurrences and takes max of valid xs; Coq defines the same condition via is_valid and maximality.
Both define ans as -1 when no positive x has count >= x, otherwise as the maximum such x. Python counts occurrences and selects max valid x, matching the Coq spec.

Both define ans as the maximum positive integer x whose own value appears at least x times in the list, or -1 if no such x exists. The Python code computes counts, filters valid x (x>0 and count>=x), returns whether ans equals max(valid) or -1 if none, matching the Coq spec.
